summary: Excellent matrix mix captures a stellar early‑Mydland performance highlighted by an epic Scarlet>Fire and a delicate Terrapin.
review: The recording delivers a clear, spacious matrix blend that lets the band’s early Brent Mydland era shine.  Highlights include a marathon Scarlet>Fire jam and a gently rendered Terrapin Station, both praised for their musical depth.  Overall the show is regarded as one of the stronger mid‑‘80s outings with superb mixing and strong group playing.

ai_rating.rationale: Consistently high reviewer scores (5/5, 4/5) emphasize both stellar performance (long Scarlet>Fire, solid Terrapin) and excellent matrix recording quality.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Matrix blend of soundboard and audience praised as clear, spacious and well‑balanced; reviewers note an "outstanding" mix with crisp instrument separation.

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set described as strong and well‑played, leading into a famously long Scarlet>Fire jam that flows seamlessly; the show closes gently with Terrapin Station.

band_member_comments.Keys: Brent Mydland delivers strong keyboard work and vocals, noted as having a good night throughout the performance.
